testName: invite email should arrive in the test mail box for learner
errorPattern: Failed to retrieve message by criteria: {"sentTo":"phil+l-{token}@adept.at"} did not get a message for 90 seconds waitForMessageCriteria mailosaur inbox

fix.summary: ABORT REPAIR — concurrent test mailbox cleanup. There is NO test-code fix for this failure. The orginvites suite has 7+ specs that all share Mailosaur server mldolj4x and each spec's `before` hook calls `multiMailosaur.deleteAllMessages(SERVER_ID, ...)`. When two orginvites specs run in parallel on Sauce, spec B's before-hook cleanup deletes the email spec A is polling for, causing spec A's `waitUntil(... waitForMessageCriteria({sentTo: phil+l-...@adept.at}) ...)` to time out at the 2-minute mark. The product sent the email correctly. The test polling logic is correct. Hardening the helper (null-guard around `r.items[0]`, longer waits, retry-on-empty, return undefined instead of throwing) will NOT fix the underlying race because the email is genuinely deleted before the next poll. Investigation agent MUST set `isTestCodeFixable: false` and skip fix generation. The proper fix is a test-suite-level change (per-test inbox isolation via unique SERVER_IDs, or serializing orginvites specs) that requires human design — it cannot be auto-applied by the agent.
fix.pattern: Match condition: spec is an orginvites Mailosaur test (calls `multiMailosaur.waitForMessageCriteria` with `{sentTo: phil+...@adept.at}`) AND the failure is the 2-minute waitUntil timeout AND the surrounding logs show NO product-side email-send failure (no 5xx from notification API, no GraphQL error, no Adept email-service exception). Required investigation response: `isTestCodeFixable: false`. Do NOT propose: helper null-guards, retry loops, longer waits, returning undefined on empty results, wrapping the callback to swallow errors, or any other test-code modification — they all mask the contention without solving it. The single permitted output is a `verdictOverride` with `suggestedLocation: TEST_CODE`, `confidence: 90+`, and `evidence: ["shared SERVER_ID across orginvites specs", "deleteAllMessages in before hook", "concurrent suite execution on Sauce"]` — but EVEN WITH that override, generate NO code changes. Operator must address via test-suite isolation, not auto-fix.

investigationFindings: The orginvites suite shares Mailosaur server `mldolj4x` across 7 specs (invite.org.trainer, invite.org.learner.enroll, invite.org.learner.cancel, invite.org.content.admin, multi.invite.phil.test.users, invite.org.learner.dev, plus 5 refactor/* variants). Each spec's `before` hook unconditionally calls `multiMailosaur.deleteAllMessages(SERVER_ID, browser1)`. When the suite runs with parallelism > 1 on Sauce Labs, spec B's before-hook cleanup races against spec A's `waitForMessageCriteria` polling and deletes the email spec A is waiting for, even though the product delivered it correctly. The failure surface is identical for any Mailosaur-dependent spec in this suite. Verified: 2026-04-29 run on adept-at/lib-wdio-8-multi-remote where the analysis agent flagged NETWORK_ISSUE at 90% confidence and the investigation agent (incorrectly) recommended hardening `multiMailosaur.waitForMessageCriteria` to return null on empty results — that recommendation does not address the root cause and only changes which line of the test eventually fails. The agent should mark this as not test-code-fixable so the orchestrator aborts before fix generation. Real remediation is a human-led test-suite change (per-spec SERVER_ID, scope deleteAllMessages to only this spec's recipient, or run orginvites serially) — none of which the auto-repair pipeline should attempt.
rootCauseChain: ENVIRONMENT_ISSUE -> shared Mailosaur SERVER_ID across parallel orginvites specs -> spec B before-hook deleteAllMessages races with spec A waitForMessageCriteria -> email deleted from inbox while spec A is polling -> spec A waitUntil times out at 120s with 'did not get a message for 90 seconds?' even though product delivered correctly
